query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
Research complete. Summary of findings on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell (as of July 10, 2026):

**Recent trend:** Foreign investors were **record net sellers of ~148.32 trillion won (~$95–96B)** on the KOSPI in H1 2026 — the largest half-year outflow on record — despite the index's >100% YTD surge. Selling accelerated in May–June (**−92.89 trillion won**) as the won weakened, peaking with a **record single-day sell-off of −7.76 trillion won on June 30**.

**Latest weekly data (week of July 6–10):** A tentative reversal. After 13 straight selling sessions (June 19–July 7, including a July 7 circuit-breaker plunge), foreigners flipped to net buying: **+343.6 billion won (July 8)** and **+134.8 billion won (July 9)** — the first consecutive buy days. The KOSPI held ~7,200 and closed the week at 7,476 (+2.52% Friday) on a chip rally.

**Drivers:** Won depreciation (USD/KRW 1,483→1,549), profit-taking/rebalancing after the ~101% surge (per KRX CEO), with selling concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix (Samsung foreign ownership at a 16-year low).

**Caveat:** The price-series index levels appeared anomalously high vs. real-world KOSPI, so I anchored the answer on the trillion-won flow figures from Korea JoongAng Daily, Seoul Economic Daily, CNBC, and Bloomingbit. Full sourced report and structured data have been delivered.

sources.snippet: Authoritative primary source for daily/weekly investor-type (foreign/institution/retail) flow data.

key_facts: Foreign investors net sold 148.32 trillion won (~$95-96 billion) of KOSPI stocks in H1 2026 (Jan-Jun), a record half-year outflow.
May-June 2026 alone saw 92.89 trillion won of net foreign selling as the won weakened.
June 30, 2026 recorded a record single-day foreign net sell-off of 7.76 trillion won.
Foreign investors net sold for 13 consecutive sessions from June 19 to July 7, 2026.
Reversal to net buying: July 8 (+343.6 billion won) and July 9 (+134.8 billion won) 2026 — first consecutive buy days after the streak.
July 7, 2026: heavy foreign selling drove KOSPI down ~4.9%, triggering a circuit breaker.
Selling concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix; Samsung foreign ownership hit a 16-year low.
Earlier June net buying: +2.204T won (Jun 12), +1.077T won (Jun 15), +1.535T won (Jun 16).
Won depreciated from 1,483.3 to 1,549.4 per dollar (May-June), a key driver of outflows.
KRX CEO Jeong Eun-bo characterized the selloff as portfolio rebalancing after a ~101% surge, not lost confidence.
KOSPI closed at 7,475.94 on July 10, 2026, up 2.52% on chip-stock gains, holding the ~7,200 line.

data_points.metric: Foreign net selling KOSPI May-June 2026 (2 months)
Foreign net selling KOSPI single day (record)
